subroutines.name: svc5_irq_check
subroutines.title: Service 5: unrecognised interrupt (CB1 dispatch)
subroutines.description:
Tests IFR bit 2 (CB1 active edge) to check for a
shift register transfer complete. If CB1 is not set,
returns A=5 to pass the service call on. If CB1 is
set, saves registers, reads the VIA ACR, clears and
restores the SR mode bits from ws_0d64, then dispatches
the TX completion callback via the operation type stored
in tx_op_type. The indexed handler performs the completion
action (e.g. resuming background print spooling) before
returning with A=0 to claim the service call.

subroutines.name: service_handler
subroutines.title: Service call dispatch
subroutines.description:
Handles service calls 1, 4, 8, 9, 13, 14, and 15.
Service 1: absolute workspace claim.
Service 4: unrecognised star command.
Service 8: unrecognised OSWORD.
Service 9: *HELP.
Service 13: ROM initialisation.
Service 14: ROM initialisation complete.
Service 15: vectors claimed.

subroutines.name: tube_addr_data_dispatch
subroutines.title: Tube address/data dispatch
subroutines.description:
Called by 10 sites across the Tube host and Econet
code. Routes requests based on the value of A:
  A < &80: data transfer setup (SENDW) at &0435
  &80 <= A < &C0: release -- maps A via ORA #&40
    and compares with tube_claimed_id; if we own
    this address, falls through to tube_release_claim
  A >= &C0: external address claim from another host
Falls through to tube_release_claim when releasing our
current claim.

subroutines.name: tube_release_claim
subroutines.title: Release Tube address claim via R4 command 5
subroutines.description:
Saves interrupt state (PHP/SEI) to protect the R4
protocol sequence, sends R4 command 5 (release) followed
by the currently-claimed address from tube_claimed_id
(&15), then restores interrupts (PLP). Falls through to
clear_tube_claim to reset the claimed-address state to
the &80 sentinel.

subroutines.name: clear_tube_claim
subroutines.title: Reset Tube address claim state
subroutines.description:
Stores &80 into both tube_claimed_id (&15) and
tube_claim_flag (&14). The &80 sentinel indicates no
address is currently claimed and no claim is in
progress. Called after tube_release_claim (via
fall-through) and during initial workspace setup.

subroutines.name: tube_begin
subroutines.title: Tube host startup entry (BEGIN)
subroutines.description:
Entry point via JMP from &0400. Enables interrupts, checks
break type via OSBYTE &FD: soft break re-initialises Tube and
restarts, hard break claims address &FF. Sends ROM contents
to co-processor page by page via SENDW, then claims the final
transfer address.

subroutines.name: tube_claim_default
subroutines.title: Claim default Tube transfer address
subroutines.description:
Sets Y=0, X=&53 (address &0053), then JMP tube_addr_claim
to initiate a Tube address claim for the default transfer
address. Called from the BEGIN startup path and after the
page transfer loop completes.

subroutines.name: tube_init_reloc
subroutines.title: Initialise relocation address for ROM transfer
subroutines.description:
Sets the Tube transfer source page to &8000
(tube_xfer_page = &80) and the page counter to &80.
Checks ROM type bit 5 for a relocation address in the
ROM header. If set, scans past the null-terminated
copyright string and extracts the 4-byte relocation
address into tube_transfer_addr (&53), tube_xfer_page
(&54), tube_xfer_addr_2 (&55), and tube_xfer_addr_3
(&56). If clear, uses the default &8000 start address.
Called twice during tube_begin: once for initial setup
and once after each page transfer completes.

subroutines.name: tube_read_string
subroutines.title: Read string from Tube R2 into buffer
subroutines.description:
Loops reading bytes from tube_read_r2 into the
string buffer at &0700, storing at string_buf+Y.
Terminates on CR (&0D) or when Y wraps to zero
(256-byte overflow). Returns with X=0, Y=7 so that
XY = &0700, ready for OSCLI or OSFIND dispatch.
Called by the Tube OSCLI and OSFIND handlers.

subroutines.name: tube_send_r2
subroutines.title: Send byte to Tube data register R2
subroutines.description:
Polls Tube status register 2 until bit 6 (TDRA)
is set, then writes A to the data register. Uses a
tight BIT/BVC polling loop. Called by 12 sites
across the Tube host code for all R2 data
transmission: command responses, file data, OSBYTE
results, and control block bytes.

subroutines.name: tube_send_r4
subroutines.title: Send byte to Tube data register R4
subroutines.description:
Polls Tube status register 4 until bit 6 is set,
then writes A to the data register. Uses a tight
BIT/BVC polling loop. R4 is the command/control
channel used for address claims (ADRR), data transfer
setup (SENDW), and release commands. Called by 7
sites, primarily during tube_release_claim and
tube_transfer_setup sequences.

subroutines.name: tube_send_r1
subroutines.title: Send byte to Tube data register R1
subroutines.description:
Polls Tube status register 1 until bit 6 is set,
then writes A to the data register. Uses a tight
BIT/BVC polling loop. R1 is used for asynchronous
event and escape notification to the co-processor.
Called by tube_event_handler to forward event type,
Y, and X parameters, and reached via BMI from
tube_escape_check when the escape flag is set.

subroutines.name: tube_read_r2
subroutines.title: Read a byte from Tube data register R2
subroutines.description:
Polls Tube status register 2 until bit 7 (RDA)
is set, then loads and returns the byte from Tube
data register 2. Uses a BIT/BPL polling loop (testing
the N flag). R2 is the primary data channel from the
co-processor. Called by 14 sites across the Tube host
code for command dispatch, OSFILE/OSGBPB control block
reads, string reads, and OSBYTE parameter reception.

subroutines.name: tube_osbput
subroutines.title: Tube OSBPUT handler (R2 cmd 8)
subroutines.description:
Reads file handle and data byte from R2, then
calls OSBPUT (&FFD4) to write the byte. Falls through
to tube_reply_ack to send &7F acknowledgement.

subroutines.name: tube_osbget
subroutines.title: Tube OSBGET handler (R2 cmd 7)
subroutines.description:
Reads file handle from R2, calls OSBGET (&FFD7)
to read a byte, then falls through to tube_rdch_reply
which encodes the carry flag (error) into bit 7 and
sends the result byte via R2.

subroutines.name: tube_osrdch
subroutines.title: Tube OSRDCH handler (R2 cmd 0)
subroutines.description:
Calls OSRDCH (&FFE0) to read a character from
the current input stream, then falls through to
tube_rdch_reply which encodes the carry flag (error)
into bit 7 and sends the result byte via R2.

subroutines.name: tube_osfind
subroutines.title: Tube OSFIND handler (R2 cmd 9)
subroutines.description:
Reads open mode from R2. If zero, reads a file
handle and closes that file. Otherwise saves the mode,
reads a filename string into &0700 via tube_read_string,
then calls OSFIND (&FFCE) to open the file. Sends the
resulting file handle (or &00) via tube_reply_byte.

subroutines.name: tube_osargs
subroutines.title: Tube OSARGS handler (R2 cmd 6)
subroutines.description:
Reads file handle from R2 into Y, then reads
a 4-byte argument and reason code into zero page.
Calls OSARGS (&FFDA), sends the result A and 4-byte
return value via R2, then returns to the main loop.

subroutines.name: tube_oscli
subroutines.title: Tube OSCLI handler (R2 cmd 1)
subroutines.description:
Reads a command string from R2 into &0700 via
tube_read_string, then calls OSCLI (&FFF7) to execute
it. Falls through to tube_reply_ack to send &7F
acknowledgement.

subroutines.name: tube_osfile
subroutines.title: Tube OSFILE handler (R2 cmd 10)
subroutines.description:
Reads a 16-byte control block into zero page,
a filename string into &0700 via tube_read_string,
and a reason code from R2. Calls OSFILE (&FFDD),
then sends the result A and updated 16-byte control
block back via R2. Returns to the main loop via mj.

subroutines.name: tube_osgbpb
subroutines.title: Tube OSGBPB handler (R2 cmd 11)
subroutines.description:
Reads a 13-byte control block and reason code
from R2 into zero page. Calls OSGBPB (&FFD1), then
sends 12 result bytes and the carry+result byte
(via tube_rdch_reply) back via R2.

subroutines.name: tube_osbyte_2param
subroutines.title: Tube OSBYTE 2-param handler (R2 cmd 2)
subroutines.description:
Reads X and A from R2, calls OSBYTE (&FFF4)
with Y=0, then sends the result X via
tube_reply_byte. Used for OSBYTE calls that take
only A and X parameters.

subroutines.name: tube_osbyte_long
subroutines.title: Tube OSBYTE 3-param handler (R2 cmd 3)
subroutines.description:
Reads X, Y, and A from R2, calls OSBYTE
(&FFF4), then sends carry+Y and X as result bytes
via R2. Used for OSBYTE calls needing all three
parameters and returning both X and Y results.

subroutines.name: tube_osword
subroutines.title: Tube OSWORD handler (R2 cmd 4)
subroutines.description:
Reads OSWORD number A and in-length from R2,
then reads the parameter block into &0128. Calls
OSWORD (&FFF1), then sends the out-length result
bytes from the parameter block back via R2.
Returns to the main loop via tube_return_main.

subroutines.name: tube_osword_rdln
subroutines.title: Tube OSWORD 0 handler (R2 cmd 5)
subroutines.description:
Handles OSWORD 0 (read line) specially. Reads
4 parameter bytes from R2 into &0128 (max length,
min char, max char, flags). Calls OSWORD 0 (&FFF1)
to read a line, then sends &7F+CR or the input line
byte-by-byte via R2, followed by &80 (error/escape)
or &7F (success).

subroutines.name: adlc_init
subroutines.title: ADLC initialisation
subroutines.description:
Initialise ADLC hardware and Econet workspace.
Reads station ID via &FE18 (INTOFF side effect),
performs a full ADLC reset (adlc_full_reset), then
checks for Tube co-processor via OSBYTE &EA and
stores the result in l0d63. Issues NMI claim service
request (OSBYTE &8F, X=&0C). Falls through to
init_nmi_workspace to copy the NMI shim to RAM.

subroutines.name: init_nmi_workspace
subroutines.title: Initialise NMI workspace (skip service request)
subroutines.description:
Copies 32 bytes of NMI shim code from ROM
(listen_jmp_hi) to &0D00, then patches the current
ROM bank number into the self-modifying code at
&0D07. Clears tx_src_net, need_release_tube, and
tx_op_type to zero. Reads station ID into tx_src_stn
(&0D22). Sets ws_0d60 and ws_0d62 to &80 to mark
TX complete and Econet initialised. Finally re-enables
NMIs via INTON (&FE20 read).

subroutines.name: nmi_rx_scout
subroutines.title: NMI RX scout handler (initial byte)
subroutines.description:
Default NMI handler for incoming scout frames. Checks if the frame
is addressed to us or is a broadcast. Installed as the NMI target
during idle RX listen mode.
Tests SR2 bit0 (AP = Address Present) to detect incoming data.
Reads the first byte (destination station) from the RX FIFO and
compares against our station ID. Reading &FE18 also disables NMIs
(INTOFF side effect).

subroutines.name: nmi_rx_scout_net
subroutines.title: RX scout second byte handler
subroutines.description:
Reads the second byte of an incoming scout (destination network).
Checks for network match: 0 = local network (accept), &FF = broadcast
(accept and flag), anything else = reject.
Installs the scout data reading loop handler at &970E.

subroutines.name: scout_error
subroutines.title: Scout error/discard handler
subroutines.description:
Handles scout reception errors and end-of-frame
conditions. Reads SR2 and tests AP|RDA (bits 0|7):
if neither set, the frame ended cleanly and is
simply discarded. If unexpected data is present,
performs a full ADLC reset. Also serves as the
common discard path for address/network mismatches
from nmi_rx_scout and scout_complete -- reached by
5 branch sites across the scout reception chain.

subroutines.name: scout_complete
subroutines.title: Scout completion handler
subroutines.description:
Processes a completed scout frame. Writes CR1=&00
and CR2=&84 to disable PSE and suppress FV, then
tests SR2 for FV (frame valid). If FV is set with
RDA, reads the remaining scout data bytes in pairs
into the buffer at &0D3D. Matches the port byte
(&0D40) against open receive control blocks to find
a listener. On match, calculates the transfer size
via tx_calc_transfer, sets up the data RX handler
chain, and sends a scout ACK. On no match or error,
discards the frame via scout_error.

subroutines.name: nmi_data_rx
subroutines.title: Data frame RX handler (four-way handshake)
subroutines.description:
Receives the data frame after the scout ACK has been sent.
First checks AP (Address Present) for the start of the data frame.
Reads and validates the first two address bytes (dest_stn, dest_net)
against our station address, then installs continuation handlers
to read the remaining data payload into the open port buffer.

Handler chain: &97E6 (AP+addr check) -> &97FA (net=0 check) ->
&9810 (skip ctrl+port) -> &9843 (bulk data read) -> &9877 (completion)

subroutines.name: install_data_rx_handler
subroutines.title: Install data RX bulk or Tube handler
subroutines.description:
Selects between the normal bulk RX handler (&8239)
and the Tube RX handler based on bit 1 of rx_src_net
(tx_flags). If normal mode, loads the handler address
&8239 and checks SR1 bit 7: if IRQ is already asserted
(more data waiting), jumps directly to nmi_data_rx_bulk
to avoid NMI re-entry overhead. Otherwise installs the
handler via set_nmi_vector and returns via RTI.

subroutines.name: nmi_error_dispatch
subroutines.title: NMI error handler dispatch
subroutines.description:
Common error/abort entry used by 12 call sites. Checks
tx_flags bit 7: if clear, does a full ADLC reset and returns
to idle listen (RX error path); if set, jumps to tx_result_fail
(TX not-listening path).

subroutines.name: nmi_data_rx_bulk
subroutines.title: Data frame bulk read loop
subroutines.description:
Reads data payload bytes from the RX FIFO and stores them into
the open port buffer at (open_port_buf),Y. Reads bytes in pairs
(like the scout data loop), checking SR2 between each pair.
SR2 non-zero (FV or other) -> frame completion at &9877.
SR2 = 0 -> RTI, wait for next NMI to continue.

subroutines.name: data_rx_complete
subroutines.title: Data frame completion
subroutines.description:
Reached when SR2 non-zero during data RX (FV detected).
Same pattern as scout completion (&9738): disables PSE (CR2=&84,
CR1=&00), then tests FV and RDA. If FV+RDA, reads the last byte.
If extra data available and buffer space remains, stores it.
Proceeds to send the final ACK via &98EE.

subroutines.name: ack_tx
subroutines.title: ACK transmission
subroutines.description:
Sends a scout ACK or final ACK frame as part of the four-way handshake.
If bit7 of &0D4A is set, this is a final ACK -> completion (&9EA8).
Otherwise, configures for TX (CR1=&44, CR2=&A7) and sends the ACK
frame (dst_stn, dst_net from &0D3D, src_stn from &FE18, src_net=0).
The ACK frame has no data payload -- just address bytes.

After writing the address bytes to the TX FIFO, installs the next
NMI handler from &0D4B/&0D4C (saved by the scout/data RX handler)
and sends TX_LAST_DATA (CR2=&3F) to close the frame.

subroutines.name: nmi_ack_tx_src
subroutines.title: ACK TX continuation
subroutines.description:
Continuation of ACK frame transmission. Reads our
station ID from &FE18 (INTOFF side effect), tests
TDRA via SR1, and writes station + network=0 to the
TX FIFO, completing the 4-byte ACK address header.
Then checks rx_src_net bit 7: if set, branches to
start_data_tx to begin the data phase. Otherwise
writes CR2=&3F (TX_LAST_DATA) and falls through to
post_ack_scout for scout processing.

subroutines.name: post_ack_scout
subroutines.title: Post-ACK scout processing
subroutines.description:
Called after the scout ACK has been transmitted. Processes the
received scout data stored in the buffer at &0D3D-&0D48.
Checks the port byte (&0D40) against open receive blocks to
find a matching listener. If a match is found, sets up the
data RX handler chain for the four-way handshake data phase.
If no match, discards the frame.

subroutines.name: advance_rx_buffer_ptr
subroutines.title: Advance RX buffer pointer after transfer
subroutines.description:
Adds the transfer count to the RXCB buffer pointer (4-byte
addition). If a Tube transfer is active, re-claims the Tube
address and sends the extra RX byte via R3, incrementing the
Tube pointer by 1.

subroutines.name: rx_complete_update_rxcb
subroutines.title: Complete RX and update RXCB
subroutines.description:
Finalises a received data transfer. Calls
advance_rx_buffer_ptr to update the 4-byte buffer
pointer with the transfer count (and handle Tube
re-claim if needed). Adds the buffer bytes remaining
to the base address, then subtracts 8 from the RXCB
buffer length to account for the scout overhead.
Clears the RXCB flag byte and sends the final ACK
via ack_tx. On Tube transfers, releases the Tube
claim before resetting to idle listen.

subroutines.name: discard_reset_listen
subroutines.title: Discard with Tube release
subroutines.description:
Checks whether a Tube transfer is active by
ANDing bit 1 of l0d63 with rx_src_net (tx_flags).
If a Tube claim is held, calls release_tube to
free it before returning. Used as the clean-up
path after RXCB completion and after ADLC reset
to ensure no stale Tube claims persist.

subroutines.name: copy_scout_to_buffer
subroutines.title: Copy scout data to port buffer
subroutines.description:
Copies scout data bytes (offsets 4-11) from the
RX scout buffer at &0D3D into the open port buffer.
Checks bit 1 of rx_src_net (tx_flags) to select the
write path: direct memory store via (open_port_buf),Y
for normal transfers, or Tube data register 3 write
for Tube transfers. Calls advance_buffer_ptr after
each byte. Falls through to release_tube on
completion. Handles page overflow (Y wrap) by
branching to scout_page_overflow.

subroutines.name: release_tube
subroutines.title: Release Tube co-processor claim
subroutines.description:
Tests need_release_tube (&98) bit 7: if set, the
Tube has already been released and the subroutine
just clears the flag. If clear (Tube claim held),
calls tube_addr_data_dispatch with A=&82 to release
the claim, then clears the release flag via LSR
(which shifts bit 7 to 0). Called after completed
RX transfers and during discard paths to ensure no
stale Tube claims persist.

subroutines.name: immediate_op
subroutines.title: Immediate operation handler (port = 0)
subroutines.description:
Checks the control byte at l0d30 for immediate
operation codes (&81-&88). Codes below &81 or above
&88 are out of range and discarded. Codes &87-&88
(HALT/CONTINUE) bypass the protection mask check.
For &81-&86, converts to a 0-based index and tests
against the immediate operation mask at &0D61 to
determine if this station accepts the operation.
If accepted, dispatches via the immediate operation
table. Builds the reply by storing data length,
station/network, and control byte into the RX buffer.

subroutines.name: rx_imm_exec
subroutines.title: RX immediate: JSR/UserProc/OSProc setup
subroutines.description:
Sets up the port buffer to receive remote procedure
data. Copies the 2-byte remote address from &0D32
into the execution address workspace at &0D66, then
jumps to the common receive path at c81c1. Used for
operation types &83 (JSR), &84 (UserProc), and
&85 (OSProc).

subroutines.name: rx_imm_poke
subroutines.title: RX immediate: POKE setup
subroutines.description:
Sets up workspace offsets for receiving POKE data.
port_ws_offset=&2E, rx_buf_offset=&0D, then jumps to
the common data-receive path at c81af.

subroutines.name: rx_imm_machine_type
subroutines.title: RX immediate: machine type query
subroutines.description:
Sets up a buffer at &88C1 (length #&01FC) for the
machine type query response. Falls through to
set_rx_buf_len_hi to configure buffer dimensions,
then branches to set_tx_reply_flag.

subroutines.name: rx_imm_peek
subroutines.title: RX immediate: PEEK setup
subroutines.description:
Writes &0D2E to port_ws_offset/rx_buf_offset, sets
scout_status=2, then calls tx_calc_transfer to send
the PEEK response data back to the requesting station.

subroutines.name: advance_buffer_ptr
subroutines.title: Increment 4-byte receive buffer pointer
subroutines.description:
Adds one to the counter at &A2-&A5 (port_buf_len
low/high, open_port_buf low/high), cascading
overflow through all four bytes. Called after each
byte is stored during scout data copy and data
frame reception to track the current write position
in the receive buffer.

subroutines.name: imm_op_build_reply
subroutines.title: Build immediate operation reply header
subroutines.description:
Stores data length, source station/network, and control byte
into the RX buffer header area for port-0 immediate operations.
Then disables CB1 interrupts and configures the VIA shift
register for outgoing shift-out mode before returning to
idle listen.

subroutines.name: tx_done_jsr
subroutines.title: TX done: remote JSR execution
subroutines.description:
Pushes address &857A on the stack (so RTS returns to
tx_done_exit), then does JMP (l0d66) to call the remote
JSR target routine. When that routine returns via RTS,
control resumes at tx_done_exit.

subroutines.name: tx_done_os_proc
subroutines.title: TX done: OSProc call
subroutines.description:
Calls the ROM service entry point with X=l0d66,
Y=l0d67. This invokes an OS-level procedure on
behalf of the remote station, then exits via
tx_done_exit.

subroutines.name: tx_done_halt
subroutines.title: TX done: HALT
subroutines.description:
Sets bit 2 of rx_flags (&0D61), enables interrupts,
and spin-waits until bit 2 is cleared (by a CONTINUE
from the remote station). If bit 2 is already set,
skips to exit.

subroutines.name: tx_done_continue
subroutines.title: TX done: CONTINUE
subroutines.description:
Clears bit 2 of rx_flags (&0D61), releasing any
station that is halted and spinning in tx_done_halt.

subroutines.name: tx_begin
subroutines.title: Begin TX operation
subroutines.description:
Main TX initiation entry point (called via trampoline at &06CE).
Copies dest station/network from the TXCB to the scout buffer,
dispatches to immediate op setup (ctrl >= &81) or normal data
transfer, calculates transfer sizes, copies extra parameters,
then enters the INACTIVE polling loop.

subroutines.name: inactive_poll
subroutines.title: INACTIVE polling loop
subroutines.description:
Entry point for the Econet line idle detection
loop. Saves the TX index in rx_remote_addr, pushes
two timeout counter bytes onto the stack, and loads
Y=&E7 (CR2 value for TX preparation). Loads the
INACTIVE bit mask (&04) into A and falls through to
intoff_test_inactive to begin polling SR2 with
interrupts disabled.

subroutines.name: intoff_test_inactive
subroutines.title: Disable NMIs and test INACTIVE
subroutines.description:
Disables NMIs via two reads of &FE18 (INTOFF),
then polls SR2 for the INACTIVE bit (bit 2). If
INACTIVE is detected, reads SR1 and writes CR2=&67
to clear status, then tests CTS (SR1 bit 4): if
CTS is present, branches to tx_prepare to begin
transmission. If INACTIVE is not set, re-enables
NMIs via &FE20 (INTON) and decrements the 3-byte
timeout counter on the stack. On timeout, falls
through to tx_line_jammed.

subroutines.name: tx_line_jammed
subroutines.title: TX timeout error handler (Line Jammed)
subroutines.description:
Reached when the INACTIVE polling loop times
out without detecting a quiet line. Writes
CR2=&07 (FC_TDRA|2_1_BYTE|PSE) to abort the TX
attempt, pulls the 3-byte timeout state from the
stack, and stores error code &40 ('Line Jammed')
in the TX control block via store_tx_error.

subroutines.name: tx_prepare
subroutines.title: TX preparation
subroutines.description:
Configures the ADLC for frame transmission.
Writes CR2=Y (&E7: RTS|CLR_TX_ST|CLR_RX_ST|FC_TDRA|
2_1_BYTE|PSE) and CR1=&44 (RX_RESET|TIE) to enable
TX with interrupts. Installs the nmi_tx_data handler
at &86E0. Sets need_release_tube flag via SEC/ROR.
Writes the 4-byte destination address (dst_stn,
dst_net, src_stn, src_net=0) to the TX FIFO. For
Tube transfers, claims the Tube address; for direct
transfers, sets up the buffer pointer from the TXCB.

subroutines.name: tx_ctrl_peek
subroutines.title: TX ctrl: PEEK transfer setup
subroutines.description:
Sets scout_status=3, then performs a 4-byte addition
of bytes from the TX block into the transfer parameter
workspace at &0D1E-&0D21 (with carry propagation).
Calls tx_calc_transfer to finalise, then exits via
tx_ctrl_exit.

subroutines.name: tx_ctrl_poke
subroutines.title: TX ctrl: POKE transfer setup
subroutines.description:
Sets scout_status=2 and shares the 4-byte addition
and transfer calculation path with tx_ctrl_peek.

subroutines.name: tx_ctrl_proc
subroutines.title: TX ctrl: JSR/UserProc/OSProc setup
subroutines.description:
Sets scout_status=2 and calls tx_calc_transfer
directly (no 4-byte address addition needed for
procedure calls). Shared by operation types &83-&85.

subroutines.name: nmi_tx_data
subroutines.title: NMI TX data handler
subroutines.description:
Writes 2 bytes per NMI invocation to the TX FIFO at &FEA2. Uses the
BIT instruction on SR1 to test TDRA (V flag = bit6) and IRQ (N flag = bit7).
After writing 2 bytes, checks if the frame is complete. If more data,
tests SR1 bit7 (IRQ) via BMI -- if IRQ still asserted, writes 2 more bytes
without returning from NMI (tight loop). Otherwise returns via RTI.

subroutines.name: tx_last_data
subroutines.title: TX_LAST_DATA and frame completion
subroutines.description:
Signals end of TX frame by writing CR2=&3F (TX_LAST_DATA). Then installs
the TX completion NMI handler at &9D14 (nmi_tx_complete).
CR2=&3F = 0011_1111:
  bit5: CLR_RX_ST -- clears fv_stored_ (prepares for RX of reply)
  bit4: TX_LAST_DATA -- tells ADLC this is the final data byte
  bit3: FLAG_IDLE -- send flags/idle after frame
  bit2: FC_TDRA -- force clear TDRA
  bit1: 2_1_BYTE -- two-byte transfer mode
  bit0: PSE -- prioritised status enable
Note: NO CLR_TX_ST (bit6=0), NO RTS (bit7=0 -- drops RTS after frame)

subroutines.name: nmi_tx_complete
subroutines.title: TX completion: switch to RX mode
subroutines.description:
Called via NMI after the frame (including CRC and closing flag) has been
fully transmitted. Switches from TX mode to RX mode by writing CR1=&82.
CR1=&82 = 1000_0010: TX_RESET | RIE (listen for reply).
Checks workspace flags to decide next action:
  - bit6 set at &0D4A -> tx_result_ok at &9EA8
  - bit0 set at &0D4A -> handshake_await_ack at &9E50
  - Otherwise -> install nmi_reply_scout at &9D30

subroutines.name: nmi_reply_scout
subroutines.title: RX reply scout handler
subroutines.description:
Handles reception of the reply scout frame after transmission.
Checks SR2 bit0 (AP) for incoming data, reads the first byte
(destination station) and compares to our station ID via &FE18
(which also disables NMIs as a side effect).

subroutines.name: nmi_reply_cont
subroutines.title: RX reply continuation handler
subroutines.description:
Reads the second byte of the reply scout (destination network) and
validates it is zero (local network). Installs nmi_reply_validate
(&9D5B) for the remaining two bytes (source station and network).
Optimisation: checks SR1 bit7 (IRQ still asserted) via BMI at &9D53.
If IRQ is still set, falls through directly to &9D5B without an RTI,
avoiding NMI re-entry overhead for short frames where all bytes arrive
in quick succession.

subroutines.name: nmi_reply_validate
subroutines.title: RX reply validation (Path 2 for FV/PSE interaction)
subroutines.description:
Reads the source station and source network from the reply scout and
validates them against the original TX destination (&0D20/&0D21).
Sequence:
  1. Check SR2 bit7 (RDA) at &9D5B -- must see data available
  2. Read source station at &9D60, compare to &0D20 (tx_dst_stn)
  3. Read source network at &9D68, compare to &0D21 (tx_dst_net)
  4. Check SR2 bit1 (FV) at &9D72 -- must see frame complete
If all checks pass, the reply scout is valid and the ROM proceeds
to send the scout ACK (CR2=&A7 for RTS, CR1=&44 for TX mode).

subroutines.name: nmi_scout_ack_src
subroutines.title: TX scout ACK: write source address
subroutines.description:
Continuation of the TX-side scout ACK. Reads our
station ID from &FE18 (INTOFF), tests TDRA via SR1,
and writes station + network=0 to the TX FIFO. Then
checks bit 1 of rx_src_net to select between the
immediate-op data NMI handler and the normal
nmi_data_tx handler at &87E4. Installs the chosen
handler via set_nmi_vector. Shares the tx_check_tdra
entry at &87BD with ack_tx.

subroutines.name: nmi_data_tx
subroutines.title: TX data phase: send payload
subroutines.description:
Transmits the data payload of a four-way
handshake. Loads bytes from (open_port_buf),Y or
from Tube R3 depending on the transfer mode, writing
pairs to the TX FIFO. After each pair, decrements
the byte count (port_buf_len). If the count reaches
zero, branches to tx_last_data to signal end of
frame. Otherwise tests SR1 bit 7 (IRQ): if still
asserted, writes another pair without returning from
NMI (tight loop optimisation). If IRQ clears, returns
via RTI.

subroutines.name: handshake_await_ack
subroutines.title: Four-way handshake: switch to RX for final ACK
subroutines.description:
Called via JMP from nmi_tx_complete when bit 0 of
&0D4A is set (four-way handshake in progress). Writes
CR1=&82 (TX_RESET|RIE) to switch the ADLC from TX
mode to RX mode, listening for the final ACK from the
remote station. Installs the nmi_final_ack handler at
&887A via set_nmi_vector.

subroutines.name: nmi_final_ack
subroutines.title: RX final ACK handler
subroutines.description:
Receives the final ACK in a four-way handshake. Same validation
pattern as the reply scout handler (&9D30-&9D5B):
  &9E5C: Check AP, read dest_stn, compare to our station
  &9E70: Check RDA, read dest_net, validate = 0
  &9E84: Check RDA, read src_stn/net, compare to TX dest
  &9EA3: Check FV for frame completion
On success, stores result=0 at tx_result_ok. On failure, error &41.

subroutines.name: nmi_final_ack_validate
subroutines.title: Final ACK validation
subroutines.description:
Continuation of nmi_final_ack. Tests SR2 for RDA,
then reads the source station and source network
bytes from the RX FIFO, comparing each against the
original TX destination at tx_dst_stn (&0D20) and
tx_dst_net (&0D21). Finally tests SR2 bit 1 (FV)
for frame completion. Any mismatch or missing FV
branches to tx_result_fail. On success, falls
through to tx_result_ok.

subroutines.name: tx_result_ok
subroutines.title: TX completion handler
subroutines.description:
Loads A=0 (success) and branches unconditionally to
tx_store_result (BEQ is always taken since A=0). This
two-instruction entry point exists so that JMP sites
can target the success path without needing to set A.
Called from ack_tx (&82EC) for final-ACK completion
and from nmi_tx_complete (&8732) for immediate-op
completion where no ACK is expected.

subroutines.name: tx_result_fail
subroutines.title: TX failure: not listening
subroutines.description:
Loads error code &41 (not listening) and falls through to
tx_store_result. The most common TX error path — reached from
11 sites across the final-ACK validation chain when the remote
station doesn't respond or the frame is malformed.

subroutines.name: tx_store_result
subroutines.title: TX result store and completion
subroutines.description:
Stores the TX result code (in A) at offset 0 of
the TX control block via (nmi_tx_block),Y=0. Sets
ws_0d60 to &80 to signal TX completion to the
foreground polling loop. Then jumps to
discard_reset_rx for a full ADLC reset and return
to idle RX listen mode.

subroutines.name: tx_calc_transfer
subroutines.title: Calculate transfer size
subroutines.description:
Computes the data transfer byte count from the
RXCB buffer pointers. Reads the 4-byte buffer end
address from (port_ws_offset) and checks for Tube
addresses (&FExx/&FFxx). For Tube transfers, claims
the Tube address and sets the transfer flag in
rx_src_net. Subtracts the buffer start from the
buffer end to compute the byte count, storing it in
port_buf_len/port_buf_len_hi. Also copies the buffer
start address to open_port_buf for the RX/TX handlers
to use as their working pointer.

subroutines.name: adlc_full_reset
subroutines.title: ADLC full reset
subroutines.description:
Performs a full ADLC hardware reset. Writes
CR1=&C1 (TX_RESET|RX_RESET|AC) to put both TX and
RX sections in reset with address control enabled.
Then configures CR4=&1E (8-bit RX word, abort extend,
NRZ encoding) and CR3=&00 (no loopback, no AEX, NRZ,
no DTR). Falls through to adlc_rx_listen to re-enter
RX listen mode.

subroutines.name: adlc_rx_listen
subroutines.title: Enter RX listen mode
subroutines.description:
Configures the ADLC for passive RX listen mode.
Writes CR1=&82 (TX_RESET|RIE): TX section held in
reset, RX interrupts enabled. Writes CR2=&67
(CLR_TX_ST|CLR_RX_ST|FC_TDRA|2_1_BYTE|PSE) to clear
all pending status and enable prioritised status.
This is the idle state where the ADLC listens for
incoming scout frames via NMI.

subroutines.name: wait_idle_and_reset
subroutines.title: Wait for idle NMI state and reset Econet
subroutines.description:
Service 12 handler: NMI release. Checks ws_0d62
to see if Econet has been initialised; if not, skips
straight to adlc_rx_listen. Otherwise spins in a
tight loop comparing the NMI handler vector at
&0D0C/&0D0D against the address of nmi_rx_scout
(&80B3). When the NMI handler returns to idle, falls
through to save_econet_state to clear the initialised
flags and re-enter RX listen mode.

subroutines.name: save_econet_state
subroutines.title: Reset Econet flags and enter RX listen
subroutines.description:
Disables NMIs via two reads of &FE18 (INTOFF),
then clears ws_0d60 (TX complete) and ws_0d62
(Econet initialised) by storing the current A value.
Sets Y=5 (service call workspace page) and jumps to
adlc_rx_listen to configure the ADLC for passive
listening. Used during NMI release (service 12) to
safely tear down the Econet state before another
ROM can claim the NMI workspace.

subroutines.name: nmi_bootstrap_entry
subroutines.title: Bootstrap NMI entry point (in ROM)
subroutines.description:
An alternate NMI handler that lives in the ROM itself rather than
in the RAM workspace at &0D00. Unlike the RAM shim (which uses a
self-modifying JMP to dispatch to different handlers), this one
hardcodes JMP nmi_rx_scout (&96BF). Used as the initial NMI handler
before the workspace has been properly set up during initialisation.
Same sequence as the RAM shim: BIT &FE18 (INTOFF), PHA, TYA, PHA,
LDA romsel, STA &FE30, JMP &96BF.

subroutines.name: rom_set_nmi_vector
subroutines.title: ROM copy of set_nmi_vector + nmi_rti
subroutines.description:
A version of the NMI vector-setting subroutine and RTI sequence
that lives in ROM. The RAM workspace copy at &0D0E/&0D14 is the
one normally used at runtime; this ROM copy is used during early
initialisation before the RAM workspace has been set up, and as
the source for the initial copy to RAM.

subroutines.name: scan_remote_keys
subroutines.title: Scan keyboard for remote operation keys
subroutines.description:
Uses OSBYTE &7A with Y=&7F to check whether
remote operation keys (&CE-&CF) are currently
pressed. If neither key is detected, clears
svc_state and nfs_workspace to zero via the
clear_svc_and_ws entry point, which is also used
directly by cmd_roff. Called by check_escape.

subroutines.name: save_text_ptr
subroutines.title: Save OS text pointer for later retrieval
subroutines.description:
Copies &F2/&F3 into fs_crc_lo/fs_crc_hi. Called by
svc_4_star_command and svc_9_help before attempting
command matches, and by match_fs_cmd during
iterative help topic matching. Preserves A via
PHA/PLA.

subroutines.name: print_cmd_table
subroutines.title: Print *HELP command listing with optional header
subroutines.description:
If V flag is set, saves X/Y, calls
print_version_header to show the ROM version
string and station number, then restores X/Y.
If V flag is clear, outputs a newline only.
Either path then falls through to
print_cmd_table_loop to enumerate commands.

subroutines.name: print_cmd_table_loop
subroutines.title: Enumerate and print command table entries
subroutines.description:
Walks the ANFS command table from offset X,
printing each command name padded to 9 characters
followed by its syntax description. Entries with
bit 7 set mark end-of-table. The syntax descriptor
byte's low 5 bits index into cmd_syntax_table;
index &0E triggers special handling that lists
shared command names in parentheses. Calls
help_wrap_if_serial to handle line continuation
on serial output streams. Preserves Y.

subroutines.name: help_wrap_if_serial
subroutines.title: Wrap *HELP syntax lines for serial output
subroutines.description:
Checks the output destination via &0355. Returns
immediately for VDU (stream 0) or printer
(stream 3) output. For serial streams, outputs a
newline followed by 12 spaces of indentation to
align continuation lines with the syntax
description column.

subroutines.name: print_version_header
subroutines.title: Print ANFS version string and station number
subroutines.description:
Uses an inline string after JSR print_inline:
CR + "Advanced  4.08.53" + CR. After the inline
string, JMPs to print_station_id to append the
local Econet station number.

subroutines.name: get_ws_page
subroutines.title: Read workspace page number for current ROM slot
subroutines.description:
Indexes into the MOS per-ROM workspace table at
&0DF0 using romsel_copy (&F4) as the ROM slot.
Returns the allocated page number in both A and Y
for caller convenience.

subroutines.name: setup_ws_ptr
subroutines.title: Set up zero-page pointer to workspace page
subroutines.description:
Calls get_ws_page to read the page number, stores
it as the high byte in nfs_temp (&CD), and clears
the low byte at &CC to zero. This gives a
page-aligned pointer used by FS initialisation and
cmd_net_fs to access the private workspace.

subroutines.name: notify_new_fs
subroutines.title: Notify OS of filing system selection
subroutines.description:
Calls FSCV with A=6 to announce the FS change,
then issues paged ROM service call 10 via OSBYTE
&8F to inform other ROMs. Sets X=&0A and branches
to issue_svc_osbyte which falls through from the
call_fscv subroutine.

subroutines.name: call_fscv
subroutines.title: Dispatch to filing system control vector (FSCV)
subroutines.description:
Indirect JMP through FSCV at &021E, providing
OS-level filing system services such as FS
selection notification (A=6) and *RUN handling.
Also contains issue_svc_15 and issue_svc_osbyte
entry points that issue paged ROM service requests
via OSBYTE &8F.

subroutines.name: check_credits_easter_egg
subroutines.title: Easter egg: match *HELP keyword to author credits
subroutines.description:
Matches the *HELP argument against a keyword
embedded in the credits data at
credits_keyword_start. Starts matching from offset
5 in the data (X=5) and checks each byte against
the command line text until a mismatch or X reaches
&0D. On a full match, prints the ANFS author
credits string: B Cockburn, J Dunn, B Robertson,
and J Wills, each terminated by CR.

subroutines.name: clear_if_station_match
subroutines.title: Clear stored station if parsed argument matches
subroutines.description:
Parses a station number from the command line via
init_bridge_poll and compares it with the expected
station at &0E01 using EOR. If the parsed value
matches (EOR result is zero), clears &0E01. Called
by cmd_iam when processing a file server address
in the logon command.

subroutines.name: osbyte_x0_y0
subroutines.title: OSBYTE wrapper with X=0, Y=0
subroutines.description:
Sets X=0 and Y=0 then branches to jmp_osbyte.
Called from the Econet OSBYTE dispatch chain to
handle OSBYTEs that require both X and Y cleared.
The unconditional BEQ (after LDY #0 sets Z)
reaches the JMP osbyte instruction at &8E71.

subroutines.name: store_ws_page_count
subroutines.title: Record workspace page count (capped at &21)
subroutines.description:
Stores the workspace allocation from service 1
into offset &0F of the receive control block,
capping the value at &21 to prevent overflow into
adjacent workspace areas. Called by
svc_2_private_workspace after issuing the absolute
workspace claim service call.

subroutines.name: init_adlc_and_vectors
subroutines.title: Initialise ADLC and install extended vectors
subroutines.description:
Reads the ROM pointer table via OSBYTE &A8,
writes vector addresses and ROM ID into the
extended vector table for NETV and one additional
vector, then restores any previous FS context.

subroutines.name: write_vector_entry
subroutines.title: Install extended vector table entries
subroutines.description:
Copies vector addresses from the dispatch table at
svc_dispatch_lo_offset+Y into the MOS extended
vector table pointed to by fs_error_ptr. For each
entry, writes address low, high, then the current
ROM ID from romsel_copy (&F4). Loops X times.
After the loop, stores &FF at &0D72 as an
installed flag, calls deselect_fs_if_active and
get_ws_page to restore FS state.

on_entry.y: starting offset in extended vector table

subroutines.name: restore_fs_context
subroutines.title: Restore FS context from saved workspace
subroutines.description:
Copies 8 bytes (offsets 6 to &0D) from the saved
workspace at &0DFA back into the receive control
block via (net_rx_ptr). This restores the station
identity, directory handles, and library path after
a filing system reselection. Called by
svc_2_private_workspace during init,
deselect_fs_if_active during FS teardown, and
flip_set_station_boot.

subroutines.name: fscv_6_shutdown
subroutines.title: Deselect filing system and save workspace
subroutines.description:
If the filing system is currently selected
(bit 7 of &0D6C set), closes all open FCBs,
closes SPOOL/EXEC files via OSBYTE &77,
saves the FS workspace to page &10 shadow
with checksum, and clears the selected flag.

subroutines.name: verify_ws_checksum
subroutines.title: Verify workspace checksum integrity
subroutines.description:
Sums bytes 0 to &76 of the workspace page via the
zero-page pointer at &CC/&CD and compares with the
stored value at offset &77. On mismatch, raises a
'net checksum' error via error_bad_inline.
Preserves A, Y, and processor flags using PHP/PHA.
Called by 5 sites across format_filename_field,
adjust_fsopts_4bytes, and start_wipe_pass before
workspace access.

subroutines.name: print_station_id
subroutines.title: Print Econet station number and clock status
subroutines.description:
Uses print_inline to output 'Econet Station ',
then reads the station ID from offset 5 of the
receive control block and prints it as a decimal
number via print_num_no_leading. Tests ADLC
status register 2 (&FEA1) to detect the Econet
clock; if absent, appends ' No Clock' via a
second inline string. Finishes with OSNEWL.
Called by print_version_header and svc_3_auto_boot.

subroutines.name: print_hex_byte
subroutines.title: Print A as two hexadecimal digits
subroutines.description:
Saves A on the stack, shifts right four times
to isolate the high nybble, calls
print_hex_nybble to print it, then restores
the full byte and falls through to
print_hex_nybble for the low nybble. Called by
print_5_hex_bytes, cmd_ex, cmd_dump, and
print_dump_header.

subroutines.name: print_hex_nybble
subroutines.title: Print low nybble of A as hex digit
subroutines.description:
Masks A to the low 4 bits, then converts to
ASCII: adds 7 for letters A-F (via ADC #6 with
carry set from the CMP), then ADC #&30 for the
final '0'-'F' character. Outputs via JMP OSASCI.

subroutines.name: parse_addr_arg
subroutines.title: Parse decimal or hex station address argument
subroutines.description:
Reads from the command argument at (&BE),Y.
Supports '&' prefix for hex, '.' separator for
net.station addresses, and plain decimal.
Returns result in A. Raises errors for
bad digits, overflow, or zero values.

subroutines.name: is_decimal_digit
subroutines.title: Test for digit, '&', or '.' separator
subroutines.description:
Compares A against '&' and '.' first; if
either matches, returns with carry set via the
shared return_12 exit. Otherwise falls through
to is_dec_digit_only for the '0'-'9' range
test. Called by cmd_iam, cmd_ps, and
cmd_pollps when parsing station addresses.

subroutines.name: is_dec_digit_only
subroutines.title: Test for decimal digit '0'-'9'
subroutines.description:
Uses two CMPs to bracket-test A against the
range &30-&39. CMP #&3A sets carry if A >= ':'
(above digits), then CMP #&30 sets carry if
A >= '0'. The net effect: carry set only for
'0'-'9'. Called by parse_addr_arg.

subroutines.name: get_access_bits
subroutines.title: Read and encode directory entry access byte
subroutines.description:
Loads the access byte from offset &0E of the
directory entry via (fs_options),Y, masks to 6
bits (AND #&3F), then sets X=4 and branches to
begin_prot_encode to map through the protection
bit encode table at &9272. Called by
check_and_setup_txcb for owner and public access.

subroutines.name: get_prot_bits
subroutines.title: Encode protection bits via lookup table
subroutines.description:
Masks A to 5 bits (AND #&1F), sets X=&FF to
start at table index 0, then enters the shared
encoding loop at begin_prot_encode. Shifts out
each source bit and ORs in the corresponding
value from prot_bit_encode_table (&9272). Called
by send_txcb_swap_addrs and check_and_setup_txcb.

subroutines.name: set_text_and_xfer_ptr
subroutines.title: Set OS text pointer then transfer parameters
subroutines.description:
Stores X/Y into the MOS text pointer at
&F2/&F3, then falls through to set_xfer_params
and set_options_ptr to configure the full FS
transfer context. Called by byte_to_2bit_index.

subroutines.name: set_xfer_params
subroutines.title: Set FS transfer byte count and source pointer
subroutines.description:
Stores A into fs_last_byte_flag (&BD) as the
transfer byte count, and X/Y into fs_crc_lo/hi
(&BE/&BF) as the source data pointer. Falls
through to set_options_ptr to complete the
transfer context setup. Called by 5 sites across
cmd_ex, format_filename_field, and gsread_to_buf.

subroutines.name: set_options_ptr
subroutines.title: Set FS options pointer and clear escape flag
subroutines.description:
Stores X/Y into fs_options/fs_block_offset
(&BB/&BC) as the options block pointer. Then
enters clear_escapable which uses PHP/LSR/PLP
to clear bit 0 of the escape flag at &97 without
disturbing processor flags. Called by
format_filename_field and send_and_receive.

subroutines.name: cmp_5byte_handle
subroutines.title: Compare 5-byte handle buffers for equality
subroutines.description:
Loops X from 4 down to 1, comparing each byte
of l00af+X with fs_load_addr_3+X using EOR.
Returns on the first mismatch (Z=0) or after
all 5 bytes match (Z=1). Called by
send_txcb_swap_addrs and check_and_setup_txcb
to verify station/handle identity.

subroutines.name: set_conn_active
subroutines.title: Set connection-active flag in channel table
subroutines.description:
Saves registers on the stack, recovers the
original A from the stack via TSX/LDA &0102,X,
then calls attr_to_chan_index to find the channel
slot. ORs bit 6 (&40) into the channel status
byte at &1060+X. Preserves A, X, and processor
flags via PHP/PHA/PLA/PLP. Called by
format_filename_field and adjust_fsopts_4bytes.

subroutines.name: clear_conn_active
subroutines.title: Clear connection-active flag in channel table
subroutines.description:
Mirror of set_conn_active but ANDs the channel
status byte with &BF (bit 6 clear mask) instead
of ORing. Uses the same register-preservation
pattern: PHP/PHA/TSX to recover A, then
attr_to_chan_index to find the slot. Shares the
done_conn_flag exit with set_conn_active.

subroutines.name: check_not_ampersand
subroutines.title: Reject '&' as filename character
subroutines.description:
Loads the first character from the parse buffer
at &0E30 and compares with '&' (&26). Branches
to error_bad_filename if matched, otherwise
returns. Also contains read_filename_char which
loops reading characters from the command line
into the TX buffer at &0F05, calling
strip_token_prefix on each byte and terminating
on CR. Used by cmd_fs_operation and cmd_rename.

subroutines.name: copy_fs_cmd_name
subroutines.title: Copy matched command name to TX buffer
subroutines.description:
Scans backwards in cmd_table_fs from the
current position to find the bit-7 flag byte
marking the start of the command name. Copies
each character forward into the TX buffer at
&0F05 until the next bit-7 byte (end of name),
then appends a space separator. Called by
cmd_fs_operation and cmd_rename.

subroutines.name: parse_quoted_arg
subroutines.title: Parse possibly-quoted filename argument
subroutines.description:
Reads from the command line at (&BE),Y. Handles
double-quote delimiters and stores the result
in the parse buffer at &0E30. Raises 'Bad string'
on unbalanced quotes.

subroutines.name: init_txcb_bye
subroutines.title: Initialise TXCB for bye/receive on port &90
subroutines.description:
Loads A=&90 (the FS command port) and falls
through to init_txcb_port, which initialises
the TXCB from the template, sets the port,
data start offset to 3, and decrements the
control byte. Called by recv_and_process_reply.

subroutines.name: init_txcb_port
subroutines.title: Initialise TXCB with specified port number
subroutines.description:
Calls init_txcb to copy the 12-byte template
into the TXCB workspace at &00C0, then stores A
as the transmit port (txcb_port at &C1), sets
txcb_start to 3 (data begins at offset 3 in the
packet), and decrements txcb_ctrl. Called by
check_and_setup_txcb.

subroutines.name: init_txcb
subroutines.title: Initialise TX control block from ROM template
subroutines.description:
Copies 12 bytes from txcb_init_template (&9477)
into the TXCB workspace at &00C0. For the first
two bytes (Y=0,1), also copies the destination
station/network from &0E00 into txcb_dest (&C2).
Preserves A via PHA/PLA. Called by 4 sites
including cmd_pass, init_txcb_port,
prep_send_tx_cb, and send_wipe_request.

subroutines.name: send_request_nowrite
subroutines.title: Send read-only FS request (carry set)
subroutines.description:
Pushes A and sets carry to indicate no-write
mode, then branches to txcb_copy_carry_set to
enter the common TXCB copy, send, and reply
processing path. The carry flag controls whether
a disconnect is sent on certain reply codes.
Called by setup_transfer_workspace.

subroutines.name: send_request_write
subroutines.title: Send read-write FS request (V clear)
subroutines.description:
Clears V flag and branches unconditionally to
txcb_copy_carry_clr (via BVC, always taken after
CLV) to enter the common TXCB copy, send, and
reply processing path with carry clear (write
mode). Called by do_fs_cmd_iteration and
send_txcb_swap_addrs.

subroutines.name: save_net_tx_cb
subroutines.title: Save FS state and send command to file server
subroutines.description:
Copies station address and function code (Y)
to the TX buffer, builds the TXCB, sends the
packet, and waits for the reply. V is clear
for standard mode.

subroutines.name: save_net_tx_cb_vset
subroutines.title: Save and send TXCB with V flag set
subroutines.description:
Variant of save_net_tx_cb for callers that have
already set V. Copies the FS station address
from &0E02 to &0F02, then falls through to
txcb_copy_carry_clr which clears carry and enters
the common TXCB copy, send, and reply path.
Called by check_and_setup_txcb,
format_filename_field, and cmd_remove.

subroutines.name: prep_send_tx_cb
subroutines.title: Build TXCB from scratch, send, and receive reply
subroutines.description:
Full send/receive cycle: saves flags, sets
reply port &90, calls init_txcb to load the
template, computes txcb_end from X+5, then
dispatches based on carry: C set sends a
disconnect via handle_disconnect, C clear calls
init_tx_ptr_and_send and falls through to
recv_and_process_reply. Called by
setup_transfer_workspace.

subroutines.name: recv_and_process_reply
subroutines.title: Receive FS reply and dispatch on status codes
subroutines.description:
Calls init_txcb_bye to set up a receive TXCB
on port &90, then wait_net_tx_ack to wait for
the acknowledgment. Iterates over reply bytes:
zero terminates, V-set codes are adjusted by
+&2B, and non-zero codes dispatch to
store_reply_status. Handles disconnect requests
(C set from prep_send_tx_cb) and 'Data Lost'
warnings when channel status bits indicate
pending writes were interrupted.

subroutines.name: check_escape
subroutines.title: Check for pending escape condition
subroutines.description:
ANDs the MOS escape flag (&FF) with the
escapable flag at &97. If bit 7 of the result
is clear (no escape or escape disabled), returns
immediately. Otherwise enters raise_escape_error:
acknowledges the escape via OSBYTE &7E, then
jumps to classify_reply_error with A=6 to raise
the Escape error. Called by cmd_pass and
send_net_packet.

subroutines.name: wait_net_tx_ack
subroutines.title: Wait for Econet TX completion with timeout
subroutines.description:
Saves the timeout counter from &0D6F and the
TX control state from &0D61, then polls
net_tx_ptr_hi (&9B) for completion. Uses a
three-level nested loop: the outer counter
comes from the configured timeout at &0D6F.
On completion, restores both saved values.
On timeout (all loops exhausted), branches to
build_no_reply_error to raise 'No reply'.
Called by 6 sites across the protocol stack.

subroutines.name: cond_save_error_code
subroutines.title: Conditionally store error code to workspace
subroutines.description:
Tests bit 7 of &0D6C (FS selected flag). If
clear, returns immediately. If set, stores A
into &0E09 as the last error code. This guards
against writing error state when no filing system
is active. Called internally by the error
classification chain and by error_inline_log.

subroutines.name: append_drv_dot_num
subroutines.title: Append 'net.station' decimal string to error text
subroutines.description:
Reads network and station numbers from the TX
control block at offsets 3 and 2. Writes a space
separator then the network number (if non-zero),
a dot, and the station number as decimal digits
into the error text buffer at the current position.

on_exit.x: updated buffer index past appended text

subroutines.name: append_space_and_num
subroutines.title: Append space and decimal number to error text
subroutines.description:
Writes a space character to the error text buffer
at the current position (fs_load_addr_2), then falls
through to append_decimal_num to convert the value
in A to decimal digits with leading zero suppression.

subroutines.name: append_decimal_num
subroutines.title: Convert byte to decimal and append to error text
subroutines.description:
Extracts hundreds, tens and units digits by three
successive calls to append_decimal_digit. Uses the
V flag to suppress leading zeros — hundreds and tens
are skipped when zero, but the units digit is always
emitted.

subroutines.name: append_decimal_digit
subroutines.title: Extract and append one decimal digit
subroutines.description:
Divides Y by A using repeated subtraction to extract
a single decimal digit. Stores the ASCII digit in the
error text buffer at fs_load_addr_2 unless V is set
and the quotient is zero (leading zero suppression).
Returns the remainder in Y for subsequent digit
extraction.

on_exit.v: clear once a non-zero digit is emitted

subroutines.name: init_tx_ptr_and_send
subroutines.title: Point TX at zero-page TXCB and send
subroutines.description:
Sets net_tx_ptr/net_tx_ptr_hi to &00C0 (the
standard TXCB location in zero page), then falls
through to send_net_packet for transmission with
retry logic.

subroutines.name: send_net_packet
subroutines.title: Transmit Econet packet with retry
subroutines.description:
Polls for line idle, starts transmission via
the ADLC, and retries on failure with a
configurable count and delay. Enables escape
handling after the first retry phase exhausts
its count.

subroutines.name: init_tx_ptr_for_pass
subroutines.title: Set up TX pointer and send pass-through packet
subroutines.description:
Copies the template into the TX buffer (skipping
&FD markers), saves original values on stack,
then polls the ADLC and retries until complete.

subroutines.name: setup_pass_txbuf
subroutines.title: Initialise TX buffer from pass-through template
subroutines.description:
Copies 12 bytes from pass_txbuf_init_table into the
TX control block, pushing the original values on the
stack for later restoration. Skips offsets marked &FD
in the template. Starts transmission via
poll_adlc_tx_status and retries on failure, restoring
the original TX buffer contents when done.

subroutines.name: poll_adlc_tx_status
subroutines.title: Poll ADLC and start frame transmission
subroutines.description:
Shifts the workspace status byte left in a
loop, then copies the TX pointer into the NMI
TX block and calls tx_begin to start frame
transmission. Returns the TX completion status
in A.

subroutines.name: load_text_ptr_and_parse
subroutines.title: Copy text pointer from FS options and parse string
subroutines.description:
Reads a 2-byte address from (fs_options)+0/1 into
os_text_ptr (&00F2), resets Y to zero, then falls
through to gsread_to_buf to parse the string at that
address into the &0E30 buffer.

subroutines.name: gsread_to_buf
subroutines.title: Parse command line via GSINIT/GSREAD into &0E30
subroutines.description:
Calls GSINIT to initialise string reading, then
loops calling GSREAD to copy characters into the
l0e30 buffer until end-of-string. Appends a CR
terminator and sets fs_crc_lo/hi to point at &0E30
for subsequent parsing routines.

subroutines.name: do_fs_cmd_iteration
subroutines.title: Execute one iteration of a multi-step FS command
subroutines.description:
Called by match_fs_cmd for commands that enumerate
directory entries. Sets port &92, sends the initial
request via send_request_write, then synchronises the
FS options and workspace state (order depends on the
cycle flag at offset 6). Copies 4 address bytes,
formats the filename field, sends via
send_txcb_swap_addrs, and receives the reply.

subroutines.name: send_txcb_swap_addrs
subroutines.title: Send TXCB and swap start/end addresses
subroutines.description:
If the 5-byte handle matches, returns
immediately. Otherwise sets port &92, copies
addresses, sends, waits for acknowledgment,
and retries on address mismatch.

subroutines.name: print_load_exec_addrs
subroutines.title: Print exec address and file length in hex
subroutines.description:
Prints the exec address as 5 hex bytes from
(fs_options) offset 9 downwards, then the file
length as 3 hex bytes from offset &0C. Each group
is followed by a space separator via OSASCI.

subroutines.name: print_5_hex_bytes
subroutines.title: Print hex byte sequence from FS options
subroutines.description:
Outputs X+1 bytes from (fs_options) starting at
offset Y, decrementing Y for each byte (big-endian
display order). Each byte is printed as two hex
digits via print_hex_byte. Finishes with a trailing
space via OSASCI. The default entry with X=4 prints
5 bytes (a full 32-bit address plus extent).

subroutines.name: copy_fsopts_to_zp
subroutines.title: Copy FS options address bytes to zero page
subroutines.description:
Copies 4 bytes from (fs_options) at offsets 2-5
into zero page at &00AE+Y. Used by
do_fs_cmd_iteration to preserve the current address
state. Falls through to skip_one_and_advance5 to
advance Y past the copied region.

subroutines.name: skip_one_and_advance5
subroutines.title: Advance Y by 5
subroutines.description:
Entry point one INY before advance_y_by_4, giving
a total Y increment of 5. Used to skip past a
5-byte address/length structure in the FS options
block.

subroutines.name: advance_y_by_4
subroutines.title: Advance Y by 4
subroutines.description:
Four consecutive INY instructions. Used as a
subroutine to step Y past a 4-byte address field
in the FS options or workspace structure.

subroutines.name: copy_workspace_to_fsopts
subroutines.title: Copy workspace reply data to FS options
subroutines.description:
Copies bytes from the reply buffer at &0F02+Y
into (fs_options) at offsets &0D down to 2. Used
to update the FS options block with data returned
from the file server. Falls through to
retreat_y_by_4.

subroutines.name: retreat_y_by_4
subroutines.title: Retreat Y by 4
subroutines.description:
Four consecutive DEY instructions. Companion to
advance_y_by_4 for reverse traversal of address
structures.

subroutines.name: retreat_y_by_3
subroutines.title: Retreat Y by 3
subroutines.description:
Three consecutive DEY instructions. Used by
setup_transfer_workspace to step back through
interleaved address pairs in the FS options block.

subroutines.name: check_and_setup_txcb
subroutines.title: Set up data transfer TXCB and dispatch reply
subroutines.description:
Compares the 5-byte handle; if unchanged,
returns. Otherwise computes start/end addresses
with overflow clamping, sets the port and control
byte, sends the packet, and dispatches on the
reply sub-operation code.

subroutines.name: format_filename_field
subroutines.title: Format filename into fixed-width display field
subroutines.description:
Builds a 12-character space-padded filename at
&10F3 for directory listing output. Sources the
name from either the command line or the l0f05
reply buffer depending on the value in l0f03.
Truncates or pads to exactly 12 characters.

subroutines.name: update_addr_from_offset9
subroutines.title: Update both address fields in FS options
subroutines.description:
Calls add_workspace_to_fsopts for offset 9 (the
high address / exec address field), then falls
through to update_addr_from_offset1 to process
offset 1 (the low address / load address field).

subroutines.name: update_addr_from_offset1
subroutines.title: Update low address field in FS options
subroutines.description:
Sets Y=1 and falls through to
add_workspace_to_fsopts to add the workspace
adjustment bytes to the load address field at
offset 1 in the FS options block.

subroutines.name: add_workspace_to_fsopts
subroutines.title: Add workspace bytes to FS options with clear carry
subroutines.description:
Clears carry and falls through to
adjust_fsopts_4bytes. Provides a convenient entry
point when the caller needs addition without a
preset carry.

on_entry.y: FS options offset for first byte

subroutines.name: adjust_fsopts_4bytes
subroutines.title: Add or subtract 4 workspace bytes from FS options
subroutines.description:
Processes 4 consecutive bytes at (fs_options)+Y,
adding or subtracting the corresponding workspace
bytes from &0E0A-&0E0D. The direction is controlled
by bit 7 of fs_load_addr_2: set for subtraction,
clear for addition. Carry propagates across all 4
bytes for correct multi-byte arithmetic.

on_entry.y: FS options offset for first byte

subroutines.name: lookup_cat_entry_0
subroutines.title: Look up channel from FS options offset 0
subroutines.description:
Loads the channel handle from (fs_options) at
offset 0, then falls through to lookup_cat_slot_data
to find the corresponding FCB entry.

subroutines.name: lookup_cat_slot_data
subroutines.title: Look up channel and return FCB flag byte
subroutines.description:
Calls lookup_chan_by_char to find the channel
slot for handle A in the channel table, then
loads the FCB flag byte from &1030+X.

subroutines.name: setup_transfer_workspace
subroutines.title: Prepare workspace for OSGBPB data transfer
subroutines.description:
Orchestrates the setup for OSGBPB (get/put
multiple bytes) operations. Looks up the channel,
copies the 6-byte address structure from FS options
(skipping the hole at offset 8), determines transfer
direction from the operation code (even=read,
odd=write), selects port &91 or &92 accordingly,
and sends the FS request. Then configures the TXCB
address pairs for the actual data transfer phase
and dispatches to the appropriate handler.

subroutines.name: write_data_block
subroutines.title: Write data block to destination or Tube
subroutines.description:
If no Tube present, copies directly from
the l0f05 buffer via (fs_crc_lo). If Tube
is active, claims the Tube, sets up the
transfer address, and writes via R3.

subroutines.name: tube_claim_c3
subroutines.title: Claim the Tube via protocol &C3
subroutines.description:
Loops calling tube_addr_data_dispatch with
protocol byte &C3 until the claim succeeds
(carry set on return). Used before Tube data
transfers to ensure exclusive access to the
Tube co-processor interface.

subroutines.name: print_fs_info_newline
subroutines.title: Print station address and newline
subroutines.description:
Sets V (suppressing leading-zero padding on
the network number) then prints the station
address followed by a newline via OSNEWL.
Used by *FS and *PS output formatting.

subroutines.name: parse_fs_ps_args
subroutines.title: Parse station address from *FS/*PS arguments
subroutines.description:
Reads a station address in 'net.station' format
from the command line, with the network number
optional (defaults to local network). Calls
init_bridge_poll to ensure the bridge routing
table is populated, then validates the parsed
address against known stations.

subroutines.name: get_pb_ptr_as_index
subroutines.title: Convert parameter block pointer to table index
subroutines.description:
Reads the first byte from the OSWORD parameter
block pointer and falls through to
byte_to_2bit_index to produce a 12-byte-aligned
table index in Y.

subroutines.name: byte_to_2bit_index
subroutines.title: Convert byte to 12-byte-aligned table index
subroutines.description:
Computes Y = A * 6 (via A*12/2) for indexing
into the OSWORD handler workspace tables.
Clamps Y to zero if the result exceeds &48,
preventing out-of-bounds access.

subroutines.name: match_fs_cmd
subroutines.title: Match command name against FS command table
subroutines.description:
Case-insensitive compare of the command line
against table entries with bit-7-terminated
names. Returns with the matched entry address
on success.

subroutines.name: find_station_bit2
subroutines.title: Find printer server station in table (bit 2)
subroutines.description:
Scans the 16-entry station table for a slot
matching the current station/network address
with bit 2 set (printer server active). Sets V
if found, clears V if not. Falls through to
allocate or update the matching slot with the
new station address and status flags.

subroutines.name: find_station_bit3
subroutines.title: Find file server station in table (bit 3)
subroutines.description:
Scans the 16-entry station table for a slot
matching the current station/network address
with bit 3 set (file server active). Sets V
if found, clears V if not. Falls through to
allocate or update the matching slot with the
new station address and status flags.

subroutines.name: flip_set_station_boot
subroutines.title: Set boot option for a station in the table
subroutines.description:
Scans up to 16 station table entries for one
matching the current address with bit 4 set
(boot-eligible). Stores the requested boot type
in the matching entry and calls
restore_fs_context to re-establish the filing
system state.

subroutines.name: osword_setup_handler
subroutines.title: Push OSWORD handler address for RTS dispatch
subroutines.description:
Indexes the OSWORD dispatch table by X to
push a handler address (hi then lo) onto the
stack. Copies 3 bytes from the osword_flag
workspace into the RX buffer, loads PB byte 0
(the OSWORD sub-code), and clears svc_state.
The subsequent RTS dispatches to the pushed
handler address.

subroutines.name: bin_to_bcd
subroutines.title: Convert binary byte to BCD
subroutines.description:
Uses decimal mode (SED) with a count-up loop:
starts at BCD 0 and adds 1 in decimal mode for
each decrement of the binary input. Saves and
restores the processor flags to avoid leaving
decimal mode active. Called 6 times by
save_txcb_and_convert for clock date/time
conversion.

subroutines.name: store_osword_pb_ptr
subroutines.title: Store OSWORD parameter block pointer+1 to workspace
subroutines.description:
Computes PB pointer + 1 and stores the resulting
16-bit address at workspace offset &1C via
store_ptr_at_ws_y. Then reads PB byte 1 (the
transfer length) and adds the PB low byte to
compute the buffer end pointer, stored at
workspace offset &20.

subroutines.name: store_ptr_at_ws_y
subroutines.title: Store 16-bit pointer at workspace offset Y
subroutines.description:
Writes a 16-bit address to (nfs_workspace)+Y.
The low byte comes from A; the high byte is
computed from osword_pb_ptr_hi plus carry,
supporting pointer arithmetic across page
boundaries.

subroutines.name: copy_pb_byte_to_ws
subroutines.title: Conditionally copy parameter block byte to workspace
subroutines.description:
If carry is set, loads a byte from the OSWORD
parameter block at offset Y; if clear, uses
the value already in A. Stores the result to
workspace at the current offset. Decrements X
and loops until the requested byte count is
transferred.

on_entry.c: set to load from PB, clear to use A

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_station
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 0: read file server station
subroutines.description:
Returns the current file server station and
network numbers in PB[1..2]. If the NFS is not
active (l0d6c bit 7 clear), returns zero in
PB[0] instead.

subroutines.name: osword_13_set_station
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 1: set file server station
subroutines.description:
Sets the file server station and network
numbers from PB[1..2]. Processes all FCBs,
then scans the 16-entry FCB table to
reassign handles matching the new station.
If the NFS is not active, returns zero.

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_csd
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 12: read CSD path
subroutines.description:
Reads 5 current selected directory path bytes
from the RX workspace at offset &1B into
PB[1..5]. Sets carry clear to select the
workspace-to-PB copy direction.

subroutines.name: osword_13_write_csd
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 13: write CSD path
subroutines.description:
Writes 5 current selected directory path bytes
from PB[1..5] into the RX workspace at offset
&1B. Sets carry to select the PB-to-workspace
copy direction.

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_ws_pair
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 2: read workspace byte pair
subroutines.description:
Reads 2 bytes from the NFS workspace page
starting at offset 1 into PB[1..2]. Uses
nfs_workspace_hi as the page and
copy_pb_byte_to_ws with carry clear for the
workspace-to-PB direction.

subroutines.name: osword_13_write_ws_pair
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 3: write workspace byte pair
subroutines.description:
Writes 2 bytes from PB[1..2] into the NFS
workspace at offsets 2 and 3. Then calls
init_bridge_poll and conditionally clears
the workspace byte if the bridge status
changed.

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_prot
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 4: read protection mask
subroutines.description:
Returns the current protection mask (ws_0d68)
in PB[1].

subroutines.name: osword_13_write_prot
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 5: write protection mask
subroutines.description:
Sets the protection mask from PB[1] via
store_prot_mask.

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_handles
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 6: read FCB handle info
subroutines.description:
Returns the 3-byte FCB handle/port data from
l1071[1..3] in PB[1..3]. If the NFS is not
active, returns zero in PB[0].

subroutines.name: osword_13_set_handles
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 7: set FCB handles
subroutines.description:
Validates and assigns up to 3 FCB handles
from PB[1..3]. Each handle value (&20-&2F)
indexes the l1010/l1040 tables. For valid
handles with bit 2 set in l1040, stores the
station to l0e01+Y and FCB index to l1071+Y,
then updates flag bits across all FCB entries
via update_fcb_flag_bits.

subroutines.name: update_fcb_flag_bits
subroutines.title: Update FCB flag bits across all entries
subroutines.description:
Scans all 16 FCB entries in l1060. For each
entry with bit 6 set, tests the Y-specified
bit mask: if matching, ORs bit 5 into the
flags; if not, leaves bit 5 clear. In both
cases, inverts and clears the tested bits.
Preserves X.

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_rx_flag
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 8: read RX control block flag
subroutines.description:
Returns byte 5 of the current RX control
block in PB[1].

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_rx_port
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 9: read RX port byte
subroutines.description:
Returns byte &7F of the current RX control
block in PB[1], and stores &80 in PB[2].

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_error
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 10: read error flag
subroutines.description: Returns the error flag (l0e09) in PB[1].

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_context
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 11: read context byte
subroutines.description: Returns the context byte (l0d6d) in PB[1].

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_free_bufs
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 14: read free buffer count
subroutines.description:
Returns the free buffer count (&6F minus
l0d6b) in PB[1].

subroutines.name: osword_13_read_ctx_3
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 15: read 3 context bytes
subroutines.description:
Copies 3 bytes from l0d6d[1..3] into
PB[1..3].

subroutines.name: osword_13_write_ctx_3
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 16: write 3 context bytes
subroutines.description:
Copies 3 bytes from PB[1..3] into
l0d6d[1..3].

subroutines.name: osword_13_bridge_query
subroutines.title: OSWORD &13 sub 17: query bridge status
subroutines.description:
Calls init_bridge_poll, then returns the
bridge status. If l0d72 is &FF (no bridge),
stores 0 in PB[0]. Otherwise stores l0d72
in PB[1] and conditionally updates PB[3]
based on station comparison.

subroutines.name: init_bridge_poll
subroutines.title: Initialise Econet bridge routing table
subroutines.description:
Checks the bridge status byte: if &FF
(uninitialised), broadcasts a bridge query
packet and polls for replies. Each reply
adds a network routing entry to the bridge
table. Skips the broadcast if the table has
already been populated from a previous call.

subroutines.name: enable_irq_and_poll
subroutines.title: Enable interrupts and send Econet packet
subroutines.description:
Executes CLI to re-enable interrupts, then
falls through to send_net_packet. Used after
a sequence that ran with interrupts disabled
to ensure the packet is sent with normal
interrupt handling active.

subroutines.name: push_osword_handler_addr
subroutines.title: Push OSWORD handler address for RTS dispatch
subroutines.description:
Indexes the OSWORD handler dispatch table
using the current OSWORD number to push the
handler's address (hi/lo) onto the stack.
Reloads the OSWORD number from osbyte_a_copy
so the dispatched handler can identify the
specific call.

subroutines.name: osword_4_handler
subroutines.title: OSWORD 4 handler: clear carry and send abort
subroutines.description:
Clears the carry flag in the stacked processor
status, stores the original Y to workspace at
offset &DA, and falls through to tx_econet_abort
with A=0. Called via OSWORD handler dispatch
table for OSWORD 4 (write interval timer).

subroutines.name: tx_econet_abort
subroutines.title: Send Econet abort/disconnect packet
subroutines.description:
Stores the abort code in workspace, configures
the TX control block with control byte &80
(immediate operation flag), and transmits the
abort packet. Used to cleanly disconnect from
a remote station during error recovery.

subroutines.name: match_rx_code
subroutines.title: Search receive code table for match
subroutines.description:
Scans a table of receive operation codes
starting at index X, comparing each against A.
Returns with Z set if a match is found, Z clear
if the end-of-table marker is reached.

subroutines.name: osword_8_handler
subroutines.title: OSWORD 7/8 handler: copy PB to workspace and abort
subroutines.description:
Handles OSWORD 7 or 8 by copying 15 bytes from
the parameter block to workspace at offset &DB,
storing the OSWORD number at offset &DA, setting
control value &E9, and sending an abort packet.
Returns via tx_econet_abort. Rejects other
OSWORD numbers by returning immediately.

subroutines.name: init_ws_copy_wide
subroutines.title: Initialise workspace copy in wide mode (14 bytes)
subroutines.description:
Copies 14 bytes to workspace offset &7C.
Falls through to the template-driven copy
loop which handles &FD (skip), &FE (end),
and &FC (page pointer) markers.

subroutines.name: init_ws_copy_narrow
subroutines.title: Initialise workspace copy in narrow mode (27 bytes)
subroutines.description:
Sets up a 27-byte copy to workspace offset &17,
then falls through to ws_copy_vclr_entry for
the template-driven copy loop. Used for the
compact workspace initialisation variant.

subroutines.name: ws_copy_vclr_entry
subroutines.title: Template-driven workspace copy with V clear
subroutines.description:
Processes a template byte array to initialise
workspace. Special marker bytes: &FE terminates
the copy, &FD skips the current offset, and &FC
substitutes the workspace page pointer. All
other values are stored directly to the
workspace at the current offset.

subroutines.name: reset_spool_buf_state
subroutines.title: Reset spool buffer to initial state
subroutines.description:
Sets the spool buffer pointer to &25 (first
available data position) and the control state
byte to &41 (ready for new data). Called after
processing a complete spool data block.

subroutines.name: append_byte_to_rxbuf
subroutines.title: Append byte to receive buffer
subroutines.description:
Stores A in the receive buffer at the current
buffer index (ws_ptr_lo), then increments the
index. Used to accumulate incoming spool data
bytes before processing.

subroutines.name: handle_spool_ctrl_byte
subroutines.title: Handle spool control byte and flush buffer
subroutines.description:
Rotates bit 0 of the control byte into carry
for mode selection (print vs spool), appends
the byte to the buffer, calls process_spool_data
to transmit the accumulated data, and resets
the buffer state ready for the next block.

subroutines.name: process_spool_data
subroutines.title: Transmit accumulated spool buffer data
subroutines.description:
Copies the workspace state to the TX control
block, sends a disconnect reply if the previous
transfer requires acknowledgment, then handles
the spool output sequence by setting up and
sending the pass-through TX buffer.

subroutines.name: send_disconnect_reply
subroutines.title: Send Econet disconnect reply packet
subroutines.description:
Sets up the TX pointer, copies station
addresses, matches the station in the table,
and sends the response. Waits for
acknowledgment before returning.

subroutines.name: commit_state_byte
subroutines.title: Copy current state byte to committed state
subroutines.description:
Reads the working state byte from workspace and
stores it to the committed state location. Used
to finalise a state transition after all related
workspace fields have been updated.

subroutines.name: serialise_palette_entry
subroutines.title: Serialise palette register to workspace
subroutines.description:
Reads the current logical colour for a palette
register via OSBYTE &0B and stores both the
palette value and the display mode information
in the workspace block. Used during remote
screen state capture.

subroutines.name: read_osbyte_to_ws_x0
subroutines.title: Read OSBYTE with X=0 and store to workspace
subroutines.description:
Sets X=0 then falls through to read_osbyte_to_ws
to issue the OSBYTE call and store the result.
Used when the OSBYTE parameter X must be zero.

subroutines.name: read_osbyte_to_ws
subroutines.title: Issue OSBYTE from table and store result
subroutines.description:
Loads the OSBYTE function code from the next
